data.title: C C Land Sets 2026 AGM to Approve Accounts, Board Changes and Share Issue Mandate
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

C C Land Holdings ( (HK:1224) ) has provided an update.

C C Land Holdings has called its annual general meeting for 7 May 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025, consider the re-election of two executive directors and one independent non-executive director, and approve director and auditor remuneration. Investors will also vote on a general mandate allowing the board to issue up to 20% of the company’s share capital, plus any shares repurchased under a separate buyback mandate, a step that provides management with additional flexibility for future capital raising or corporate actions while potentially diluting existing holdings if fully exercised.

More about C C Land Holdings

C C Land Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in Bermuda and traded under stock code 1224. It operates in the property and investment sector, with its activities and governance overseen by a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors, and its financial reporting audited by Ernst & Young.

Average Trading Volume: 54,042

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: HK$4.66B

data.title: Canadian Solar Infrastructure Fund Sees Limited Impact From Fukuyama Plant Outage
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Canadian Solar Infrastructure Fund.Inc. ( (JP:9284) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Canadian Solar Infrastructure Fund has reported a complete halt in power generation at its CS Fukuyama-shi Power Plant following a power conditioning system failure on April 10, 2026. The plant, with 3.31 MW of capacity, stopped producing electricity that evening and remains offline as the operator works to identify the cause and carry out restoration.

The fund expects the outage to have only a limited impact on overall rent income because the affected plant represents 1.34% of its total panel output and is contractually entitled to receive basic rent equivalent to 70% of the forecast monthly rent. CSIF therefore does not plan to revise its earnings forecasts for the fiscal periods ending June 2026 and December 2026, signaling that the incident should not materially affect its financial outlook.

More about Canadian Solar Infrastructure Fund.Inc.

Canadian Solar Infrastructure Fund, Inc. is a Japanese-listed investment corporation focused on owning and operating a portfolio of solar power plants. Its assets comprise 35 photovoltaic facilities across Japan with a combined panel output of about 247.57 MW, generating rental income through long-term lease arrangements with power plant operators.

Average Trading Volume: 878

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: Yen42.01B

An update from TravelSky Technology ( (HK:0696) ) is now available.

TravelSky Technology Limited has announced the current composition of its board of directors, led by chairman and executive director Jiang Bo, and including non-executive, independent non-executive and employee representative directors. The company also detailed the structure of its four board committees covering audit, remuneration and evaluation, nomination, and strategy and investment (legal compliance), indicating the respective chairmanships and memberships as part of its corporate governance disclosure.

The allocation of directors across these committees underscores TravelSky’s emphasis on oversight, risk control and strategic planning as it manages its role in China’s aviation IT market. Clear designation of responsibilities, particularly in audit and legal compliance, is likely intended to reassure investors and other stakeholders about the robustness of its governance framework and decision-making processes.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0696) stock is a Buy with a HK$14.70 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on TravelSky Technology stock, see the HK:0696 Stock Forecast page.

More about TravelSky Technology

TravelSky Technology Limited is a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China and listed under stock code 00696. The company operates in the aviation and travel technology sector, providing information technology solutions and related services to airlines, airports and travel agents, supporting China’s air travel infrastructure and distribution systems.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

CRH plc ( (CRH) ) has provided an update.

CRH plc has repurchased and redeemed 100 of its ordinary shares in the U.S. market on 10 April 2026 via Wells Fargo Securities, at a volume-weighted average price of $117.64, with the shares to be cancelled. The transactions form part of a previously announced share buyback program of up to $300 million running to 28 April 2026, leaving CRH with 668.2 million ordinary shares in issue and 35.6 million held in treasury, a move that marginally reduces the free float and may enhance earnings per share over time for remaining investors.

More about CRH plc

CRH plc is a global building materials group that supplies products for construction and infrastructure projects. The company operates across multiple regions, with its ordinary shares traded in the U.S. market, and focuses on delivering value to shareholders through capital allocation initiatives including share buybacks.

data.description:

The latest announcement is out from Viridis Mining and Minerals Limited ( (AU:VMM) ).

Viridis Mining and Minerals Limited has disclosed a change in director Timothy Joseph Harrison’s indirect interests in the company’s securities, held through Timel Holdings Pty Ltd. The notice outlines adjustments to his holdings of fully paid ordinary shares, options, and performance rights in line with ASX reporting requirements.

Harrison exercised 200,000 unquoted options at an exercise price of $0.40, converting them into 200,000 fully paid ordinary shares for a total consideration of $80,000. Following this transaction, his indirect holding increased to 250,000 fully paid ordinary shares while his unquoted options position was reduced to zero, a move that marginally increases his equity stake and aligns his interests more closely with shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:VMM) stock is a Buy with a A$4.30 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Viridis Mining and Minerals Limited stock, see the AU:VMM Stock Forecast page.

Viridis Mining and Minerals Limited operates in the mining and minerals sector, focusing on the exploration and development of mineral resources. BAIOO Family Interactive ( (HK:2100) ) just unveiled an update.

BAIOO Family Interactive Limited announced that its executive director and chairman, Mr. Dai Jian, has increased his stake in the company through market purchases conducted via Stmoritz Investment Limited. On 10 April 2026, Stmoritz acquired 488,000 ordinary shares at an average price of HK$0.481 per share, bringing its total holding to 689,908,180 shares, or about 24.1% of the company’s issued share capital.

The company disclosed that between 30 March and 10 April 2026, Mr. Dai bought a cumulative 1,964,000 shares on the market through Stmoritz. This continued accumulation by the chairman signals management’s confidence in the company’s prospects and may be interpreted by investors as a positive indicator for BAIOO’s valuation and future performance.

More about BAIOO Family Interactive

BAIOO Family Interactive Limited is a Cayman Islands–incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating in the interactive entertainment sector. Atomos ( (AU:AMS) ) has issued an announcement.

Atomos Limited has notified the ASX of a proposed issue of up to 35,602,592 ordinary fully paid shares under a placement or other type of issue. The company has applied for these securities to be quoted on the ASX, with a proposed issue date of 31 May 2029, indicating a planned capital-raising initiative that may influence its funding capacity and capital structure.

By lodging this Appendix 3B, Atomos is signaling its intention to tap equity markets in accordance with ASX Listing Rules and related disclosure requirements. The sizeable maximum number of new shares, if fully issued, could have implications for existing shareholders through potential dilution, while providing the company with additional financial flexibility for future operational or strategic needs.

More about Atomos

Atomos Limited, listed on the ASX under the code AMS, is an Australian publicly traded company. It issues ordinary fully paid shares and operates under the ASX Listing Rules, accessing capital markets through placements and other security issues to support its corporate activities.
